name: Wild Bud
terroir: Tropical to subtropical, 1200-1800m elevation, wild forest environment
cultivar: Camellia crassicolumna (wild species, possibly ancestral to modern tea)
flavor_profile: Barely recognizable as tea — more like a wild herbal infusion. The flavor is bright, sweet, and resinous with strong pine needle character, dried apricot, and a savory-floral finish. Never becomes bitter even with extended steeping, making it extremely forgiving to brew.

brewing.rinse_hint: No rinse needed — the buds are pristine wild growth and benefit from retaining every steep.
brewing.tips: Very forgiving — almost impossible to overbrew. Higher water temps (90-95°C) bring out the resinous pine character. Lower temps (80°C) emphasize the sweet apricot side.
